purpose: Structured dossiers for the Top 10 quantum research attack directions.

progress_scale.0_10: Problem framed; no credible reproducible artifact yet.
progress_scale.11_25: Toy/proxy artifact exists and can be audited locally.
progress_scale.26_45: Reproducible baseline plus measurable delta exists, with limitations.
progress_scale.46_70: Strong baseline comparison and partial external-style validation.
progress_scale.71_90: Manuscript-grade evidence across broad benchmarks or formal targets.
progress_scale.91_100: Community-grade solution candidate; requires independent validation.

dossiers.title: Hardware-aware quantum circuit compression
dossiers.what_to_solve: Compile a quantum algorithm into fewer two-qubit gates, lower depth, lower error exposure, and a device-compatible layout while preserving auditable semantic equivalence.
dossiers.lineage: Universal quantum gate decomposition and quantum compilation; representative milestones include Barenco et al. elementary gates and SABRE-style NISQ qubit mapping.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: About 30+ years as a general compilation optimization problem; about 10 years as practical hardware-aware NISQ compilation.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Routing, commutation, measurement/reset semantics, native gate sets, device topology, and noise are coupled; exact equivalence checking becomes expensive as circuits grow.
dossiers.our_solution: Proof-logged local rewrites plus measurement-aware virtual-SWAP elimination, followed by post-virtual-SWAP 1Q resynthesis to test whether B1 can also touch non-Clifford/T-resource proxies.
dossiers.completed: 30/30 circuits rewritten; 481 virtual SWAPs and 1443 CX gates removed; 37.18% two-qubit reduction; 32.65% exposure reduction; proof replay passed 481/481. A post-virtual-SWAP 1Q pass removed 60 additional gates, reduced the logical T-count proxy 1.018x and T-depth proxy 1.015x, and passed 30/30 Aer cross-checks. Later control-RZ and U3 phase-factored diagnostics exposed additional T-resource proxy improvements. The B1/B7 gcm_h6 target selector now counts 270 arbitrary decimal rotations, and the cone-feasibility gate shows 3 target cone classes covering 111 occurrences. Strict direct CNOT-rotation-CNOT sandwiches total only 4, but cone_01 has 35 pair-local single-arbitrary windows and is the only cone class meeting B7's 30-occurrence target under that stricter local-window criterion. No rewrite, semantic certificate, or resource saving is claimed yet. The restricted cone_01 phase-removal gate then tests all 35 windows under remove-only, fixed-Z, and continuous-RZ same-envelope replacement; all three routes have 0 exact-pass windows at tolerance 1e-8, with best continuous-RZ residual 0.36435162331705345. This closes the simple phase-absorption route without proving a global obstruction theorem. The Euler-reabsorption gate then locks the arbitrary RY to 9 exact/Clifford-like candidate angles while neighboring target-qubit RZ phases reoptimize; it still finds 0 exact-pass windows, with best/median residual 0.21253656711362606 / 0.3643516233170531. The parameter-transfer obligation gate then checks all 35 cone_01 windows: every original RY(theta) has nonzero projective unitary sensitivity, 0 angles are near the pi/4 exact grid, and 4 distinct theta groups cover the windows. This does not prove a KAK lower bound or produce a rewrite, but it prevents no-carrier deletion from being counted as B7 savings. The theta-sharing ledger gate then converts those 4 groups into accounting pressure: 31 duplicate theta occurrences create an optimistic 620 proxy-T cache-reuse signal, but the accepted occurrence ledger still counts 0 removed occurrences and 0 proxy-T reduction. This is a guardrail, not a resource-saving claim. The physical cost-model gate then checks whether that cache signal can already be promoted into countable B7 savings. A follow-up shared-theta synthesis object gate defines 4 machine-readable object proposals covering all 35 windows, and a replay-verifier gate now checks 4/4 objects and 35/35 occurrences against source QASM and parameter-transfer groups with 0 mismatches. This upgrades the cost-model scaffold from 0/8 to 2/8 acceptance gates by passing CM-02 object existence and CM-03 replay. The cost model remains unaccepted: there are still no 30 occurrence-removing certificates, no layout/routing model, no factory-amortization ledger, no shared-error budget, no independent baseline, and no refreshed B7 ledger. Accepted B7 reduction remains 0.
remaining_steps: calibrated/live-like heavy-hex baseline
dynamic circuit and measurement/reset semantics

broader replayable cone_01 semantic rewrite certificate, KAK/Clifford scaffold, scoped obstruction report, or the remaining CM gates for the shared-theta physical cost model, starting with layout/routing and factory/error budgeting while explicitly carrying, sharing, or eliminating theta across at least 30 arbitrary rotation occurrences / 600 proxy-T units
broader benchmarks
independent certificate package

dossiers.title: Low-overhead quantum error correction
dossiers.what_to_solve: Reduce physical qubits, measurement rounds, and decoding resources needed to reach a target logical error rate on realistic hardware.
dossiers.lineage: Shor quantum error correction, Steane/CSS codes, Kitaev topological ideas, and Dennis-Kitaev-Landahl-Preskill topological memory.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 30 years for practical low-overhead fault tolerance.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Overhead depends on noise, decoder latency, leakage, correlated errors, measurement schedules, and layout; paper-best codes may fail under circuit-level constraints.
dossiers.our_solution: Use Wilson-bounded target-volume baselines and force every candidate code, schedule, decoder, or leakage-mitigation mechanism to beat the same denominator. Reduced-round signals are now explicitly boundary-checked; the active new route is leakage-flagged erasure information that may lower required distance without reducing syndrome rounds.
dossiers.completed: Stim/PyMatching baseline; Wilson target-volume table; biased schedule proxy; circuit-level biased-schedule sweep; same-hardware reduced-round candidate and robustness stress closed by artifact boundary; T-B2-003 leakage-flagged analytic boundary; T-B2-004 Stim HERALDED_ERASE / DEPOLARIZE1 stress with 10 d=5/d=7 improved rows; T-B2-005 false-positive overhead stress with 5 positive-false-positive d=5/d=7 rows at fp=0.001/tick and 0 improved rows by fp=0.003/tick; T-B2-006 posterior-calibrated shot-conditioned leakage boundary over 288 target comparisons and 4 calibration profiles, preserving at most 4 d=5/d=7 rows in one profile, with strict survival 0 and robust all-profile survival false; T-B2-007 posterior-weighted decoder-risk ledger over 4 risk budgets / 4608 budget-profile rows, with raw survivors 6, adjusted survivors 6/5/3/3, strict high-purity adjusted survivors 0, and robust all-profile adjusted survival false; T-B2-008 decoder-input contract feasibility gate: 10 required decoder inputs, 4 available, 6 missing; 9 feasibility gates, 4 passed, 5 failed critical gates; T-B2-009a per-shot decoder trace packet: 3 strict challenge rows, 192 shots each, 576 detector-bitstring traces, observable/prediction rows, and 482 synthetic detector/tick flag events; T-B2-009b posterior-likelihood injection gate: 3 injection profiles / 1728 profile-shots, best profile changes 0 predictions and fixes 0 failures, strong profile introduces 2 failures, improvement gate false, route demotion recommended; T-B2-009c DEM-informed detector-to-edge semantics gate: 3 semantic profiles / 1728 profile-shots, best conservative profile changes 0 predictions, fixes 0 failures, introduces 0 failures, and leaves 22 injected failures, while aggressive DEM semantics introduces 1 failure; T-B2-009d hardware-like leakage observation model gate: 3 observation profiles / 1728 profile-shots, 864 holdout profile-shots, best conservative hardware-like profile generates 415 model flag events, changes 0 predictions, fixes 0 failures, introduces 0 failures, and has holdout failure delta 0. No circuit-level production decoder, threshold, hardware, calibrated-device, quantum-advantage, or new-code claim is made.
remaining_steps: Replace the hardware-like leakage observation model with real calibrated leakage/flag observations or independent hardware traces.
Rerun posterior-likelihood decoding and require holdout improvement plus all-challenge non-regression.
Re-test d=5/d=7 rows under strict high-purity and all-profile robustness gates.
Expand distance, shots, and noise-mismatch coverage only after calibrated or hardware-derived posterior injection improves strict challenge rows.
Feed B7 only with robust non-artifact B2 evidence.
dossiers.completed_here: Stim/PyMatching rotated surface-code baseline, reduced-round artifact closure, leakage-flagged erasure analytic boundary, Stim HERALDED_ERASE stress, false-positive stress, posterior-calibrated shot-conditioned boundary, T-B2-007 posterior-weighted decoder-risk ledger, T-B2-008 decoder-input contract feasibility gate, T-B2-009a per-shot trace packet, T-B2-009b posterior-likelihood injection gate, T-B2-009c DEM-informed detector-to-edge semantics gate, and T-B2-009d hardware-like leakage observation model gate. The latest gate consumes 3 strict challenge rows across 3 observation profiles / 1728 profile-shots with 864 holdout profile-shots; best conservative hardware-like profile changes 0 predictions and fixes 0 failures, holdout failure delta remains 0, holdout improvement remains false, and route demotion remains recommended. No circuit-level decoder, production decoder, threshold, hardware, calibrated-device, quantum-advantage, or new-code claim is made.

dossiers.title: Quantum algorithms for molecular reaction dynamics
dossiers.what_to_solve: Predict reaction-path observables and dynamics with quantum algorithms, not only static small-molecule ground-state energies.
dossiers.lineage: Feynman quantum simulation, Lloyd universal quantum simulators, and Aspuru-Guzik molecular energy estimation.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: About 20 years for practical reaction-dynamics quantum advantage.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Electron correlation, active-space choice, nuclear motion, nonadiabatic effects, real-time dynamics, and observable-level errors interact; classical chemistry baselines are strong.
dossiers.our_solution: Observable-first reaction simulation that derives Hamiltonians, mappings, circuits, and resource estimates from a chosen reaction-coordinate observable.
dossiers.completed: PySCF-backed small-molecule resource proxy for H2, LiH, H2O, and N2; preliminary 6.11x-6.25x proxy reductions; B10-T1 supplies Hamiltonian-derived B3 reaction-coordinate denominators plus RHF/MP2/CCSD/FCI small-basis reference derivatives for the same four rows. T-B3-001 adds four OpenQASM observable-estimation proxy circuits aligned to those FCI derivative denominators, with max 21 qubits, max 441 controlled-phase gates, explicit measurement-shot floors, and 0 FCI denominator wins claimed. T-B3-003 replaces the proxy gap with Qiskit Nature Jordan-Wigner Hamiltonian Pauli-term measurement packets: 4 QASM packets, max 20 qubits, max 2951 mapped Pauli terms, max conservative total shot floor 30,504,129,929, state-preparation and variance costs included, and still 0 FCI wins. T-B3-004 adds sampled Pauli-estimator confidence intervals: 2048 pilot shots per random Pauli term, z=2.576 intervals all cover exact HF Pauli energy, max Neyman target shot floor 6,570,468, and 442x-34,544x reduction vs the previous conservative upper-bound shot floors; still 0 FCI wins and no selected-CI/larger-active-space denominator. T-B3-005 adds selected-CI larger-basis denominator and grouped Pauli boundary: 4 same reaction coordinates, H2/LiH cc-pVDZ and H2O/N2 3-21G selected-CI finite differences, all selected-CI points converged, max 19 spatial orbitals / 38 spin-orbital qubits, max selected determinant product 400, QWC packet reduction 1.0x-4.17x, max ansatz two-qubit executions 289,100,592, and 0 larger-basis denominator wins. T-B3-006 closes the larger-basis quantum-mapper gap: the same selected-CI denominator bases are now mapped to Jordan-Wigner qubits, with max 38 qubits, max 77,858 Pauli terms, max conservative same-basis bucket count 77,116, max Neyman target shot floor 6,464,114,739, max ansatz two-qubit executions 956,688,981,372, and still 0 larger-basis denominator wins. T-B3-007 adds actual larger-basis QWC grouping: bitmask first-fit covers reduce measurement settings 3.09x-3.96x across all four larger-basis Pauli sets, with LiH dropping from 77,116 conservative buckets to 19,644 QWC groups and max group size 291. This is a real measurement-setting reduction, but grouped covariance has not yet reduced the Neyman shot floor, so denominator wins remain 0. T-B3-008 propagates exact HF product-state covariance inside larger-basis QWC groups: grouped shot floors fall 4.91x-5.58x versus independent-term floors, max floor moves from 6,464,114,739 to 1,283,900,037, max ansatz two-qubit executions drop to 190,017,205,476, and denominator wins remain 0. T-B3-009 propagates grouped-covariance energy floors through three-point finite-difference derivative error: with delta=0.01, derivative-level shot floors inflate 10000x to a max of 12,839,000,370,000 shots. It adds UCCSD, ADAPT-VQE-envelope, and adiabatic-envelope two-qubit preparation costs; max UCCSD prep is 1,493,030 two-qubit gates and max UCCSD executions reach 18,497,517,970,970,500,000. Sampled correlated-state covariance is still unavailable and denominator wins remain 0. T-B3-010 adds the first compiled UCC/ADAPT covariance pilot: H2/cc-pVDZ one-parameter compiled UCC-double / ADAPT-seed state, 48 sampleable QWC groups, 512 shots/group, mean/max relative variance error 0.0068/0.0827, exact compiled-state center floor 66,955,026 versus HF 63,465,167, derivative floor 669,550,260,000, optimizer-loop total shots 24,773,359,620,000, optimizer-loop two-qubit executions 7,531,101,324,480,000, and still 0 denominator wins. T-B3-011 extends the pressure test across H2, LiH, H2O, and N2 using bounded high-coefficient sampled QWC subsets: 35 sampled groups total, 384 shots/group, mean/max relative variance error 0.0833/0.5029, max optimizer-loop shots lower bound 475,043,013,690,000, max optimizer-loop two-qubit lower bound 281,225,464,104,480,000, and 0 denominator wins. This demotes the current one-parameter UCC/ADAPT plus QWC route to a negative-boundary track.
remaining_steps: keep B3 demoted unless a rescue-only mechanism is proposed
produce real multi-parameter UCCSD/ADAPT covariance if continuing B3
or produce stronger-than-QWC measurement with a credible denominator win path
compare against selected-CI, DMRG, and tensor-network denominators after optimizer-loop accounting
promote only if preparation, measurement, optimizer-loop, and strong classical denominator costs are all beaten

dossiers.title: Verifiable quantum advantage protocols
dossiers.what_to_solve: Design a quantum task that a classical adversary cannot cheaply spoof and that a verifier can check under realistic noise.
dossiers.lineage: Interactive proofs, blind quantum computation, sampling advantage, and classical verification; representative milestones include UBQC and Mahadev verification.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 15-20 years for practical verifiable advantage.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Easy-to-verify tasks can be easy to fake; hard-to-simulate tasks can be hard to verify; noise and adaptive spoofers narrow soundness.
dossiers.our_solution: Hidden-trap and hidden-invariant hybrid tasks with leakage stress tests, challenge refresh, and circuit-derived projection checks.
dossiers.completed: Toy hidden-trap statistical protocol; four spoofing families rejected; B8 identifies 75% leakage as dangerous; shared B4/B8 CNOT hidden-projection refresh proxy runs 3 circuit-derived tasks / 192 configs with honest completeness 1.0, no-refresh high-leakage max soundness 0.675, and repaired high-leakage max soundness 0.0. T-B4-002a exports a B4/B8 OpenQASM 3 randomized-measurement packet with 36 hidden-projection verifier circuits, max 30 qubits including ancillas, and 0 Qiskit/Aer semantic mismatches; it is not hardware execution, hardness, advantage, or BQP separation. T-B8-003a adds a public-QASM packet spoofer boundary: public parser/emulator prediction success is 1.0 across all 36 packet circuits, so public-packet protocol soundness is rejected and private late-binding is required. T-B8-003b adds a late-bound private challenge contract: 36 public skeletons remove verifier-private masks/flips, but deterministic public data transcripts remain classically predictable, so late-bound parity challenges alone are insufficient. T-B8-003c adds a non-stabilizer late-bound transcript pilot: all 36 public skeletons receive H plus T/RZ(pi/4) challenge-basis layers, the deterministic transcript blocker is removed for 36/36 pilot circuits, minimum min-entropy is 4 bits, and maximum output probability is 0.0625; it remains an exact small-probability pilot, not hardware execution, cryptographic soundness, sampling hardness, quantum advantage, or BQP separation. T-B8-003d adds a support-aware spoofer gate: four public-support spoofer families attack all 36 non-stabilizer pilot circuits; exact transcript success remains capped at 0.0625, but support-only verifier acceptance is 1.0, so support-membership soundness is rejected.
dossiers.remaining_steps: Add verifier-private acceptance predicates, real backend properties, or hardware randomized-measurement execution beyond public support membership; attack the resulting non-public transcript; then prove completeness and soundness under explicit assumptions.

dossiers.title: Strongly correlated matter via hybrid quantum-tensor solvers
dossiers.what_to_solve: Predict strongly correlated models where classical methods disagree, especially 2D doped, finite-temperature, or dynamical settings.
dossiers.lineage: Hubbard electron-correlation model and later many-body simulation methods.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 60 years for broad strongly correlated electron problems.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Sign problem, entanglement growth, finite-size extrapolation, competing phases, and dynamical correlations.
dossiers.our_solution: Use quantum processors as entanglement or impurity kernels inside classical embedding/tensor workflows.
dossiers.completed: Exact small Hubbard reference and cluster-product proxy; cluster-4 mean error per site improves 4.63x over cluster-2. T-B5-001 adds an oracle-tuned classical boundary-field response embedding denominator on the same 9 B10 D5 Hubbard density-response rows. T-B5-002 closes the oracle-selection loophole with a non-oracle response embedding denominator: predeclared zero-field/finitesize-extrapolation policy, selected mean/max relative response error 0.05098/0.12308, 4 rows beating the oracle-tuned denominator after-the-fact, max exact D5 Hilbert dimension 4900 versus max selected cluster dimension 36, validation errors 0, and no quantum response or accuracy-per-resource win claimed. T-B5-003a adds an exact-state-seeded MPS/Schmidt truncation pressure reference on the same 9 rows: bond dimensions 2/4/8/16, selected bond dimension 16, selected mean/max relative response error 0.000442/0.001695, selected mean/max energy error per site 0.000244/0.001156, min exact-state overlap 0.999101, 6 rows beating the non-oracle embedding denominator, and explicit non-claim of variational DMRG, deployable tensor solving, quantum response, or accuracy-per-resource win. T-B5-003b adds a non-exact-state-seeded variational MPS/ALS prototype: bond dimensions 2/4, 3 restarts x 8 sweeps, selected mean/max relative response error 0.01806/0.03907, selected mean/max energy error per site 0.00303/0.00853, min exact-state overlap 0.9626, 0 rows beating the seeded MPS pressure reference, and explicit non-claim of production DMRG or quantum advantage. T-B5-004 now adds a two-site finite-DMRG-style response pressure prototype on the same 9 B10 D5 Hubbard rows: bond dimension 4, 2 restarts x 4 sweeps, selected mean/max response error 0.08196/0.27710, 4 rows beating one-site MPS/ALS, and 0 rows beating exact-state-seeded MPS pressure. It is explicitly not production DMRG, not a quantum response kernel, and not an accuracy-per-resource win. T-B5-005 adds a canonical DMRG readiness gate over the current tensor portfolio: 8 readiness gates evaluated, 0 passed and 8 failed; seeded MPS pressure remains the strongest response reference; two-site and one-site ALS prototypes each have 0 rows beating seeded pressure; prototype fixed-sector norms fail the 0.01 threshold; and production DMRG, quantum response win, accuracy-per-resource win, and same-access positive route all remain false. T-B5-006a adds a canonical-environment smoke gate over the same two-site prototype outputs: 9 environment ledgers are present, but 0 rows pass the full smoke gate, 3 rows pass the fixed-sector norm / energy variance / discarded-weight / monotonicity checks, 0 rows are close to seeded MPS pressure, 0 rows beat seeded MPS pressure, and mature canonical DMRG, production DMRG, quantum response win, accuracy-per-resource win, and same-access positive route all remain false. T-B5-006b adds a B5/B10 same-access production contract gate: current evidence passes only 2/10 gates, fails 8/10, has 0 smoke-passed rows, 0 readiness gates, 5 blocking sampling requirements, no production DMRG, no sampling oracle, and no same-access positive route.
dossiers.remaining_steps: Run T-B5-006 by implementing mature canonical-environment DMRG/MPS for the same response rows with stored environments, residuals, sweep convergence, no exact-state seeding, and full cost accounting; or compare a fully costed quantum response kernel against exact D5, non-oracle embedding, seeded MPS pressure, one-site ALS, two-site finite-DMRG-style, readiness, smoke, and production-contract denominators.

dossiers.title: High-temperature superconductivity search
dossiers.what_to_solve: Build a mechanism-aware candidate-ranking pipeline for unconventional superconductors without relying on historical family-label leakage.
dossiers.lineage: Bednorz and Muller high-Tc discovery in Ba-La-Cu-O and subsequent unconventional superconductivity research.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 40 years for predictive high-Tc mechanism and candidate search.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Huge materials space, biased data, sparse labels, descriptor leakage, and hard-to-compute strong-correlation mechanisms.
dossiers.our_solution: Mechanism-aware descriptor ranking linked to B5-derived strong-correlation observables.
dossiers.completed: Toy descriptor ranker, curated materials leakage audit, formula-derived descriptor screen, and T-B6-003 structural/electronic proxy boundary. The latest boundary keeps 38 records / 22 families / 12 expanded negatives, improves AP@12 from formula 0.10 to structural 0.611, but still loses to family-prior AP@12 1.0; post-split structural AP is 0.690 vs family-prior 0.982; top-12 contains 3 negative controls. This is not a material discovery, solved mechanism, complete database, real DFT calculation, crystallographic database pull, or computed quantum observable.
remaining_steps: replace curated/imputed structural proxies with crystallographic descriptors
attach DFT or B5-computed electronic observables
expand post-2008 negative controls and parent compounds
beat family-prior and random baselines without promoting negative controls
only then consider candidate ranking or active-learning claims

dossiers.title: Architecture-level fault-tolerance co-design
dossiers.what_to_solve: Jointly optimize algorithms, compilation, error correction, factories, layout, and scheduling to reduce fault-tolerant space-time volume.
dossiers.lineage: Shor error correction, threshold theorems, surface-code architectures, lattice surgery, and resource estimation.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 30 years for practical large-scale co-designed fault tolerance.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Circuit savings, logical error targets, factory throughput, feed-forward, routing, and layout interact nonlinearly.
dossiers.our_solution: Bridge B1 circuit savings and B2 target-volume assumptions into a workload-level dependency schedule, then add workload-DAG, factory-throughput, logical T-resource, FT synthesis, and occurrence-level rewrite-certification pressure tests.
dossiers.completed: Scalar planning model, B1/B2 dependency-schedule bridge, workload-DAG factory-throughput schedule, logical T-factory boundary tests, FT synthesis ledger, gcm_h6 min-row boundary, repeated-template/cache boundary, nonlocal template scan, w8_21 claim-boundary closure, template-priority gate, B1-side gcm_h6 target selector, B1-side cone-feasibility gate, B1-side cone_01 phase-removal gate, and B1-side cone_01 Euler-reabsorption gate. The workload-DAG schedule has mean STV reduction 1.475x; the FT synthesis ledger exposes gcm_h6 as the current min row at 1.086008x; w8_21 was tested across same-skeleton, exhaustive two-CNOT Rz/Ry, target-informed Euler-local, and bounded three-CNOT families for 43480 optimizer runs, finding 0 exact four-arbitrary-angle replacements and 0 ledger removal. The template-priority gate evaluates 12 retained templates: 0 one-angle single-template routes clear the one-sided gcm_h6 1.20x target, and best w8_21 needs at least 2 arbitrary removals per occurrence. The B1-side cone-feasibility gate shows strict direct CNOT-rotation-CNOT sandwiches total only 4, while cone_01 has 35 pair-local single-arbitrary windows and is the only target cone meeting the 30-occurrence threshold under that stricter filter. The restricted phase-removal gate tests all 35 cone_01 windows and finds 0 exact-pass windows for remove-only, fixed-Z, and continuous-RZ same-envelope replacement. The Euler-reabsorption gate also finds 0 exact-pass windows after locking RY to exact candidates and reoptimizing neighboring RZ phases. This is still not an occurrence-removing certificate, resource-saving claim, or global obstruction theorem. The parameter-transfer gate adds the sharper accounting constraint: all 35 candidate windows are sensitive to their RY(theta) values, none are pi/4-grid exact, and no-carrier deletion cannot clear the 30-window B7 target. The theta-sharing ledger gate adds the next guardrail: the 4 theta groups imply 31 duplicate theta occurrences and 620 optimistic cache-reuse proxy-T units, but the occurrence-based FT ledger still accepts 0 occurrence removal and 0 proxy-T reduction. The physical cost-model gate first rejected theta-sharing accounting at 0/8 gates. A follow-up shared-theta synthesis object gate defines 4 object proposals covering all 35 cone_01 windows, and a replay-verifier gate checks 4/4 objects and 35/35 occurrences with 0 mismatches. The updated cost-model scaffold now passes CM-02 and CM-03 and is 2/8 passed, 6/8 failed. It is still not accepted, and B7 ledger reduction remains 0.
remaining_steps: symbolic KAK/Clifford-scaffold proof, scoped obstruction, certified broader cone_01 occurrence-removing rewrite for gcm_h6 with explicit theta-carrier accounting, or the remaining CM gates for the shared-theta physical cost model after CM-02/CM-03
strengthen B1 non-Clifford/T-depth optimization until a certified occurrence-removing rewrite improves min factory STV
data-path versus T-factory claim separation
physical layout constraints
feed-forward modeling
full algorithm resource ledger

dossiers.title: Classical verification of quantum outputs
dossiers.what_to_solve: Verify task-relevant properties of quantum outputs without reconstructing the whole output distribution and without leaking hidden tests.
dossiers.lineage: Interactive proofs, property testing, blind computation, randomized measurements, and Mahadev-style verification.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 15 years for practical low-overhead verification.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Verifier sees only samples; full distributions are exponentially large; hidden invariants leak; adaptive spoofers can learn verifier structure.
dossiers.our_solution: Hidden-invariant tests plus adaptive leakage stress, followed by challenge refresh, projection rotation, and B4 circuit-derived hidden projection tasks.
dossiers.completed: Toy hidden-invariant verifier with honest completeness 1.0; five adversaries rejected; 48-configuration leakage test shows 0.75 leakage dangerous; toy refresh/rotation repair passes the <=5% high-leakage soundness gate; shared B4/B8 circuit-refresh proxy reduces high-leakage adaptive soundness from 0.675 without refresh to 0.0 under repaired modes; trained/generative spoofer stress runs 144 configs, reaches learned soundness 1.0 without refresh, and keeps projection_rotation/challenge_refresh/refresh_plus_rotation <=5% in this proxy; B10-T2 proof-obligation gate rejects no-refresh high-leakage claims; restricted refresh-independence lemma gives a 8.94e-44 single-unknown-mask bound under declared assumptions; transcript leakage simulator runs 192 configs and shows refreshed high-leakage modes retain >=6 unknown independent predicates with max empirical soundness 0.025 while no-refresh high leakage remains unsafe; device-noise transcript bridge runs 480 configs and keeps challenge_refresh/refresh_plus_rotation high-leakage soundness <=0.0208 under bounded noise, while projection_rotation is margin-sensitive and calibration side-channel leakage is rejected; Qiskit/Aer verifier bridge instantiates 216 ideal randomized parity-verifier circuits with 0 semantic mismatches, honest completeness 1.0, and up to 30 qubits including verifier ancillas; noisy Aer verifier bridge executes 9600 randomized parity-verifier circuits with honest and four adversary input families, bridge-safe noisy honest acceptance 1.0, bridge-safe noisy adversary acceptance 0.0, and at least 7 unknown independent predicates; backend-calibrated-style verifier bridge derives noise from Qiskit GenericBackendV2 target InstructionProperties and executes 5760 circuits with safe calibrated honest acceptance 1.0, safe calibrated adversary acceptance 0.25, max honest predicate-bit error 0.0703125, and no-refresh marked unsafe. T-B4-002a adds the shared OpenQASM 3 packet: 36 hardware-executable verifier circuits, all with OPENQASM 3.0 headers, max 30 qubits, and 0 Qiskit/Aer semantic mismatches. T-B8-003a adds a public-QASM packet spoofer boundary: a deterministic parser/emulator predicts all 36 public packet transcripts, public-packet spoofer acceptance is 1.0, and public-packet protocol soundness is rejected. T-B8-003b adds a late-bound private challenge contract: public skeletons hide private masks/flips for all 36 circuits, but deterministic public data transcripts remain classically predictable; 4/8 gates pass and 4/8 fail. T-B8-003c adds the non-stabilizer late-bound transcript pilot: 36/36 public skeletons now include H plus T/RZ(pi/4) challenge-basis layers, the old deterministic public-data transcript blocker is removed, minimum min-entropy is 4 bits, and maximum output probability is 0.0625; it is still exact small-probability evidence only, not hardware execution, cryptographic/protocol soundness, sampling hardness, quantum advantage, or BQP separation. T-B8-003d adds the support-aware spoofer gate: four public-support spoofer families attack all 36 non-stabilizer pilot circuits; exact transcript success remains 0.0625, but support-only verifier acceptance is 1.0, rejecting support-membership soundness while preserving the exact-transcript blocker.
dossiers.remaining_steps: Move beyond public support membership by adding verifier-private predicates, real backend properties, or hardware randomized-measurement execution; then attack that non-public transcript with stronger learned/generative spoofers.

dossiers.title: Quantum PCP and local Hamiltonian hardness
dossiers.what_to_solve: Understand whether Quantum PCP or restricted variants hold, or identify formal barriers to local-Hamiltonian gap amplification.
dossiers.lineage: Classical PCP theorem, Kitaev local Hamiltonian QMA-completeness, and the Quantum PCP conjecture.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 15-25 years for the core Quantum PCP problem.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Entanglement disrupts classical PCP intuition; gap amplification, locality, noncommuting constraints, NLTS, and quantum locally testable codes are coupled.
dossiers.our_solution: Exact small-instance lab plus counterexample database before attempting the full conjecture.
dossiers.completed: Exact small-instance gap lab with 18 configurations; nine locality-preserving candidates; zero candidate passes; four counterexample candidates. The first finite-instance failed gap-amplification negative lemma is now extracted from that lab: raw spectral-gap growth is rejected as a local Hamiltonian amplification certificate unless locality, ground-space overlap, and normalized-gap improvement are checked together. The lemma records 4 strict width-trap counterexamples, 9 dense locality traps, and 5 proof obligations, while explicitly not claiming a Quantum PCP proof or global gap-amplification impossibility. T-B9-002 adds a Lean-style symbolic skeleton with 5 definitions, 3 theorem skeletons, and the same 5 open obligations, but it is not proof-assistant checked and not a formal theorem. T-B9-003 instantiates the obstruction on `cluster_stabilizer_open_uniform_reweight`: for n=4,5,6 all terms have support 2 or 3 and are uniformly scaled by 1.35, max locality remains 3, raw gap amplifies, normalized gap is invariant, and the certificate is rejected as global energy rescaling; the available `lean` command failed and this is not a checked theorem. T-B9-004a adds a repo-local parametric certificate checker for the same family: it checks the n >= 4 formula-level term counts, support set {2,3}, max locality 3, exact uniform scale 27/20, finite rows n=4,5,6, and normalized-gap invariance by exact rational algebra. The certificate is still rejected as raw-gap-only rescaling, and it is still not a proof-assistant theorem.
remaining_steps: create a real Lean/mathlib or equivalent proof-checkable project
formalize the open-boundary cluster-stabilizer family for all n >= 4
prove support-size, uniform-scaling, spectral-width, and normalized-gap invariance lemmas
decide whether the checked statement informs a restricted theorem or remains a negative-result artifact

dossiers.title: Mapping the boundary of BQP
dossiers.what_to_solve: Separate robust quantum advantage claims from claims dependent on unrealistic data loading, oracle construction, precision, verification gaps, or noise assumptions.
dossiers.lineage: Bernstein-Vazirani quantum complexity theory, Shor algorithm, hidden subgroup work, Hamiltonian simulation, quantum walks, and sampling advantage.
dossiers.unresolved_duration: Roughly 30 years for the true boundary between BQP and classical computation.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Unconditional complexity separations are hard; oracle separations do not imply real-world advantage; input models and verification can erase speedups.
dossiers.our_solution: Reduction and failure-mode graph decomposing each advantage claim into promise, input, certificate, baseline, noise, and verification assumptions.
dossiers.completed: Reduction graph with 12 nodes, 14 edges, two connected components, eight advantage-preserving edges, six fragile edges, and 11 restricted theorem targets. Added two formal theorem target cards: B10-T1 for the HHL/data-loading negative boundary and B10-T2 for sampling-advantage verification under B4/B8 leakage and challenge-refresh assumptions. Both cards specify input, promise, output, verifier, cost-accounting, dependencies, and proof obligations, with 0 validation errors. B10-T1 now has a restricted explicit-I/O accounting lemma and corollary plus source-backed B3/B5 denominator tables. B10-T1/B5 also has oracle-tuned and non-oracle response embedding denominators on the same 9 Hubbard D5 rows: the oracle boundary-field denominator has mean/max relative response error 0.0541/0.1216, while the non-oracle predeclared zero-field/finitesize denominator has selected mean/max relative response error 0.05098/0.12308, 4 meaningful rows beating the oracle denominator after-the-fact, max exact D5 Hilbert dimension 4900 versus selected cluster dimension 36, validation errors 0, and no quantum response or accuracy-per-resource win claimed. B10-T1/B5 now also has the exact-state-seeded MPS/Schmidt truncation pressure reference: bond dimensions 2/4/8/16, selected bond dimension 16, selected mean/max relative response error 0.000442/0.001695, min exact-state overlap 0.999101, 6 rows beating the non-oracle embedding denominator, and explicit non-claim of variational DMRG or quantum advantage. B10-T1/B5 now also has the non-exact-state-seeded variational MPS/ALS prototype: bond dimensions 2/4, 3 restarts x 8 sweeps, selected mean/max relative response error 0.01806/0.03907, min exact-state overlap 0.9626, 0 rows beating the seeded pressure reference, and explicit non-claim of production DMRG or quantum advantage. B10-T2 now has a trained-spoofer minimum-refresh boundary, proof-obligation gate, restricted refresh-independence soundness lemma, transcript leakage simulator, device-noise transcript bridge, ideal Qiskit/Aer circuit-level verifier bridge, noisy Aer circuit-level verifier bridge, and backend-calibrated-style GenericBackendV2 verifier bridge from B8: no-refresh high leakage is rejected; if at least one refreshed predicate remains unknown and independent, current proxy parameters give a 8.94e-44 Hoeffding bound; in the device-noise bridge, challenge_refresh/refresh_plus_rotation keep high-leakage soundness <=0.0208 under bounded noise; in the ideal Aer bridge, 216 randomized parity-verifier circuits have 0 semantic mismatches; in the noisy Aer bridge, 9600 randomized parity-verifier circuits have bridge-safe noisy honest acceptance 1.0 and bridge-safe noisy adversary acceptance 0.0; in the backend-calibrated-style bridge, 5760 GenericBackendV2 target-property-derived noisy circuits have safe calibrated honest acceptance 1.0, adversary acceptance 0.25, and max honest predicate-bit error 0.0703125. B3 now adds T-B3-005 selected-CI larger-basis grouped Pauli boundary to the B10-T1 denominator chain; it strengthens the B3/B10 negative boundary but still gives 0 larger-basis denominator wins. B3 now also has T-B3-006 larger-basis Hamiltonian mapper boundary: the selected-CI denominator bases are mapped to qubits, but conservative measurement buckets and generic state-prep surcharge still give 0 larger-basis denominator wins. B3 now also has T-B3-007 larger-basis QWC grouping boundary: measurement-setting counts fall 3.09x-3.96x, but covariance and state-prep costs still prevent an advantage claim. B3 now also has T-B3-008 grouped covariance shot-floor boundary: HF grouped covariance lowers independent-term shot floors 4.91x-5.58x, but correlated state-prep covariance and derivative-level error propagation still prevent an advantage claim. B3 now also has T-B3-009 chemical state-prep derivative boundary: derivative propagation and UCCSD/ADAPT/adiabatic prep envelopes are explicit, but they inflate the resource ledger and still give 0 denominator wins. B3 now also has T-B3-010 compiled UCC/ADAPT covariance pilot: H2/cc-pVDZ one-parameter UCC-double / ADAPT-seed sampling runs on 48 sampleable QWC groups with mean/max variance error 0.0068/0.0827, but exact derivative and optimizer-loop accounting still give 0 denominator wins. B3 now also has T-B3-011 cross-molecule UCC/ADAPT pressure and demotion boundary: H2/LiH/H2O/N2 bounded sampled covariance pressure gives max optimizer-loop shots lower bound 475,043,013,690,000 and still 0 denominator wins, so B3 is now negative-boundary evidence in B10-T1 unless a rescue mechanism appears. T-B10-001 now adds a B3/B5 denominator boundary comparison with 4 route cards: B3 remains demoted with 0 selected-CI larger-basis denominator wins and max optimizer-loop shots lower bound 475,043,013,690,000; B5 has classical denominator pressure but 0 variational MPS/ALS rows beating seeded MPS pressure; no BQP separation or quantum advantage claim. T-B10-010 adds a missing-assumption theorem note with 2 theorem skeletons, 5 missing assumptions, and 5 proof obligations; it supports a finite negative-boundary claim policy but explicitly does not prove a dequantization theorem, sampling-access theorem, BQP separation, or quantum advantage. T-B10-011 now adds the asymptotic access-contract note: 2 family contracts for B3 reaction derivatives and B5 Hubbard response, 8 explicit/oracle/sampling/quantum access-contract rows, 5 bridge conditions, and 2 theorem targets. It refutes the sampling-access bridge for the current portfolio evidence because no comparable sampling/query oracle or positive quantum response kernel is instantiated, while explicitly not proving a general dequantization theorem, sampling-access theorem, BQP separation, or quantum advantage. T-B10-012 now adds the B5 same-access sampling-or-DMRG bridge: 4 denominator ladder rows, 5 sampling requirements all blocking, 6 seeded-MPS-over-non-oracle rows, 0 variational-over-seeded rows, no sampling oracle, no production DMRG, no same-access positive route, and explicit non-claim of dequantization, sampling theorem, BQP separation, or quantum advantage. B5/T-B5-004 now feeds the B10-T1 same-access ladder with a two-site finite-DMRG-style negative pressure reference: 4 rows beat ALS, 0 beat seeded MPS pressure, and no production-DMRG or positive route is claimed. T-B10-013 now adds an optimistic B5 response-sampler cost stress: matching exact-state-seeded MPS pressure requires min/median/max 3.861e9 / 7.645e12 / 2.849e29 shots, the max optimistic seeded-target state-preparation 2Q floor reaches 1.139e30, and 0 rows beat explicit D5 matvec ops by shots. It makes no sampling-oracle, same-access positive-route, quantum-advantage, or BQP-separation claim. T-B5-005 now adds a canonical DMRG readiness gate into the B10-T1/B5 access-model chain: 0/8 readiness gates pass, seeded MPS pressure remains strongest, non-seeded tensor references beat seeded pressure on 0 rows, and no production-DMRG, same-access positive-route, quantum-advantage, or BQP-separation claim is made. T-B5-006b now turns the B5/B10 same-access blocker into a production contract: only 2/10 gates pass, 8/10 fail, with no production DMRG, no sampling oracle, and no same-access positive route.
dossiers.remaining_steps: Run T-B10-014 with T-B5-006 by replacing the contract/cost negative boundary with mature production DMRG/MPS or a real same-access response oracle with preparation, mixing, variance, confidence, optimizer-loop, and classical denominator costs; separately push B10-T2 to real backend or hardware verifier evidence.
